title: Norinori
intro: Norinori is a logic puzzle played on a grid divided into regions.
goal: Your goal is to shade exactly two cells in each region so that every shaded cell forms part of a 1x2 domino.
board_example: An example board is shown below. Each outlined area is a region.
rule_two_per_region: Each region must contain exactly 2 shaded cells.
rule_domino: Every shaded cell must be orthogonally adjacent to exactly one other shaded cell, forming a 1x2 domino (horizontal or vertical pair).
rule_cross_region: Dominoes can span across region boundaries - the two cells of a domino don't need to be in the same region.
rule_no_2x2: No 2x2 square of shaded cells is allowed (this is automatically satisfied when all shaded cells form valid dominoes).
solved: The puzzle is solved when every region has exactly 2 shaded cells and all shaded cells form valid dominoes.
solved_board: Below is a fully solved board:
control_cycle: Left click to cycle forward, right click to cycle backward between:
control_cross: Marking the cell with an X (to indicate it won't be shaded)

compact.rule_two_per_region: Each region contains exactly 2 shaded cells.
compact.rule_domino: Every shaded cell is adjacent to exactly one other shaded cell (forming dominoes).
